title: Sports O/X
description: Rules and facts about popular sports

cards.prompt: A soccer (football) team has 11 players on the field.
cards.explanation: Each soccer team fields 11 players, including the goalkeeper.

cards.prompt: In basketball, players score by putting the ball through a hoop.
cards.explanation: Points are scored by shooting the ball through the opponent's hoop.

cards.prompt: The Olympic Games are usually held every four years.
cards.explanation: The Summer and Winter Olympics are each held every four years.

cards.prompt: A marathon is about 42 kilometres long.
cards.explanation: The official marathon distance is 42.195 kilometres.

cards.prompt: In baseball, three strikes make an out.
cards.explanation: A batter is out after three strikes.

cards.prompt: Tennis is played with a racket.
cards.explanation: Players hit the ball over a net using a racket.

cards.prompt: A soccer team has 20 players on the field at once.
cards.explanation: A soccer team has 11 players on the field, not 20.

cards.prompt: In golf, the player with the highest score wins.
cards.explanation: In golf, the lowest score (fewest strokes) wins.

cards.prompt: A basketball is smaller than a golf ball.
cards.explanation: A basketball is much larger than a golf ball.

cards.prompt: The Olympic Games are held every year.
cards.explanation: The Olympic Games are held every four years.

cards.prompt: In swimming races, athletes ride bicycles.
cards.explanation: Swimmers race by swimming; cycling is a separate sport.

cards.prompt: A volleyball team has 15 players on the court.
cards.explanation: A volleyball team has 6 players on the court.

cards.prompt: A basketball team has five players on the court at once.
cards.explanation: Each basketball team fields five players on the court at any one time.

cards.prompt: Rugby is played with a round ball.
cards.explanation: Rugby is played with an oval ball, which makes its bounce hard to predict.

cards.prompt: In tennis, a score of zero is called 'love'.
cards.explanation: Tennis uses the word 'love' to mean a score of zero, as in '15-love'.

cards.prompt: In soccer, a hat-trick means scoring five goals in one game.
cards.explanation: A hat-trick means one player scores three goals in a single game.

cards.prompt: The FIFA World Cup is held every four years.
cards.explanation: The FIFA World Cup, soccer's biggest tournament, takes place every four years.

cards.prompt: The Tour de France is a famous running race.
cards.explanation: The Tour de France is a multi-week cycling race held mainly in France.

cards.prompt: In ice hockey, players use sticks to hit a puck.
cards.explanation: Ice hockey players use sticks to shoot a flat rubber puck into the opponent's goal.

cards.prompt: In basketball, a successful shot from beyond the three-point line is worth two points.
cards.explanation: A shot made from beyond the three-point line is worth three points; shots inside the line are worth two.

cards.prompt: A boxing match takes place in a ring.
cards.explanation: Boxing matches are held in a square platform with ropes called a ring.

cards.prompt: Beach volleyball is an event at the Winter Olympics.
cards.explanation: Beach volleyball is a Summer Olympic sport; the Winter Olympics feature snow and ice sports.

cards.prompt: A standard round of golf has 18 holes.
cards.explanation: A full round of golf is played over 18 holes on the course.

cards.prompt: In bowling, knocking down all ten pins with one roll is called a spare.
cards.explanation: Knocking down all ten pins with the first roll is a strike; a spare uses two rolls.

cards.prompt: The butterfly is one of the four main swimming strokes.
cards.explanation: Competitive swimming has four main strokes: freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke, and butterfly.

cards.prompt: Cricket is played with a puck.
cards.explanation: Cricket is played with a hard ball and a bat; a puck is used in ice hockey.

cards.prompt: In baseball, a home run allows the batter to run all the bases and score.
cards.explanation: A home run, usually hit over the outfield fence, lets the batter and any runners on base score.

cards.prompt: Sumo wrestling is a traditional sport of Brazil.
cards.explanation: Sumo is the traditional national sport of Japan; Brazil is known for football and capoeira.

cards.prompt: Table tennis is also known as ping-pong.
cards.explanation: Table tennis is commonly called ping-pong, after the sound of the ball on the table and paddles.

cards.prompt: In American football, a touchdown is worth one point.
cards.explanation: A touchdown is worth six points, with the chance to add extra points afterward.

cards.prompt: A triathlon combines swimming, cycling, and running.
cards.explanation: A triathlon is a single race made up of three stages: swimming, cycling, and running.

cards.prompt: Badminton is played with a heavy metal ball.
cards.explanation: Badminton is played with a light feathered (or plastic) shuttlecock, not a ball.

cards.prompt: In soccer, the goalkeeper is the only player allowed to handle the ball during open play.
cards.explanation: Only the goalkeeper may use their hands, and only inside their own penalty area.

cards.prompt: In tennis, the ball may bounce twice before a player returns it.
cards.explanation: A player loses the point if the ball bounces twice on their side; it must be returned after at most one bounce.

cards.prompt: Fencing is a sport of sword fighting.
cards.explanation: Fencing is an Olympic combat sport in which athletes duel with blunted swords such as the foil, épée, and sabre.

cards.prompt: The 100-metre sprint is a long-distance running event.
cards.explanation: The 100-metre race is the shortest outdoor sprint; long-distance events include the 5,000 metres and beyond.
